question.label: Less grounded
question.name: When you say 'less grounded', what has changed day to day?
question.description: Drills into her phrase to surface what is underneath the shift without diagnosing it.

answer: I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.

assessment.note: She is absorbing ambiguity to shield her team, which signals meaningful strain and a mild growth signal through operating in ambiguity.

new_queue.label: Carrying ambiguity
new_queue.name: Carrying more ambiguity yourself — where is that showing up most?

new_queue.label: Support gap
new_queue.name: What support would make the biggest difference for you right now?

new_queue.label: Next step focus
new_queue.name: Given what we have covered, where do you want to focus first?

issues: coverage: injected growth into next question (0 touches after turn 5)

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
I do not think anything is broken, but I do feel less grounded than I did before.
I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
I have been protecting my team from noise, which means I am carrying more ambiguity myself.
